LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS NEW MARINA REAL ESTATE SOLUTIONS, INC. AND MIKHAIL KHUNIS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. MR. PEREZ PRESENTED ORAL ARGUMENT. UPON CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT CONFIRMED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL CLAIMS ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFFS SULTAN-HAIGHT, LLC AND SULTAN-TIBURON, LLC AND OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFF MINOO SULTAN. BECAUSE THE LLC PLAINTIFFS WERE NOT PARTIES TO THE REAL ESTATE TRANSACTIONS THAT ARE THE PREDICATE FOR THEIR CLAIMS AGAINST NEW MARINA AND KHUNIS, THEY LACK STANDING TO SUE REGARDING THOSE TRANSACTIONS. CIVIL CODE 2079.4 IS NOT THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS APPLICABLE TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT BASED ON BREACH OF A DUTY IMPOSED BY CIVIL CODE SECTIONS 2079-2079.24. NEW MARINA AND KHUNIS'S CITATION TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 339 FOR THE FIRST TIME IN THEIR REPLY BRIEF WAS NOT CONSIDERED BY THE COURT BECAUSE THAT STATUTE WAS NOT THE BASIS FOR THEIR DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike And Tax Costs
Off Calendar Mar-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS MINOO SULTAN, SULTAN-HAIGHT, LLC AND SULTAN-TIBURON, LLC'S Motion To Strike And Tax Costs claimed by defendants New Marina Real Estate Solutions and Mikhail Khunis is granted in its entirety. The costs memorandum that is the subject of this motion has not been filed with the court and no one has provided a copy to the court, so the court cannot even review what costs have been claimed. The failure to file a costs memorandum also precludes the award of any costs. (CCP 1034; CRC 3.1700). Moreover, there does not appear to have been a separate judgment entered in favor of the parties claiming costs, so the filing of a costs memorandum would be premature. Further, it appears that almost all of the claimed costs were incurred prior to the date the parties against whom the costs are claimed became parties to this case. Lastly, it appears that any costs that are allowable need to be apportioned with plaintiff Minoo Sultan and that plaintiff still has pending claims against the parties claiming costs, so any ruling on apportionment needs to wait till the conclusion of all plaintiffs' claims against all of the parties who are claiming costs. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. CAROLINE JOACHIM, ESQ. of Law Offices of Benjamin Graves, Tel No. 415-453-3600 appearing telephonically for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Zohre M. Grothe;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants New Marina Real Estate Solutions and Mikhail Khunis' demurrer to the second cause of action for negligence is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. The second cause of action is governed by a two years limitations period. By alleging that plaintiffs did not learn of the wrongs committed against them as to the SF Property until Mrs. Sultan received a March 30, 2012 letter, plaintiffs seek to avail themselves of the delayed discovery rule. However, that rule requires them to plead when they learned of the wrongs committed against them and plaintiffs do not allege whether or not Ms. Sultan received the March 30, 2012 within two years of the date this action was filed. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege that Mrs. Sultan received the March 30, 2012 letter within two years of the filing of this case. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-07-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Perez of Perez & Miller telephone number 925-284-7788 for Palintiff.
Edward McCutchan of Sunderland McCutchan, LLP telephone number 707-284-5524 appeared by telephone for Defendant.

Mr. Perez presented oral argument. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS NEW MARINA REAL ESTATE SOLUTIONS, INC. and MIKHAIL KHUNIS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to all claims alleged by plaintiffs Sultan-Haight, LLC and Sultan-Tiburon, LLC and overruled as to the second cause of action alleged by plaintiff Minoo Sultan. Because the LLC plaintiffs were not parties to the real estate transactions that are the predicate for their claims against New Marina and Khunis, they lack standing to sue regarding those transactions. Civil Code 2079.4 is not the statute of limitations applicable to the second cause of action because the second cause of action is not based on breach of a duty imposed by Civil Code sections 2079-2079.24. New Marina and Khunis's citation to Code of Civil Procedure section 339 for the first time in their reply brief was not considered by the court because that statute was not the basis for their demurrer to the second cause of action. The prevailing party is to prepare a proposed and comply with CRC 3.1312.
